speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: What had he done to make him fly the land?

speaker: ROSS
text: You must have patience, madam.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: He had none.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: His flight was madness. When our actions do not,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Our fears do make us traitors.

speaker: ROSS
text: You know not

speaker: ROSS
text: Whether it was his wisdom or his fear.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Wisdom? To leave his wife, to leave his babes,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: His mansion and his titles in a place

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: From whence himself does fly? He loves us not;

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: He wants the natural touch; for the poor wren,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: The most diminutive of birds, will fight,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Her young ones in her nest, against the owl.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: All is the fear, and nothing is the love,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: As little is the wisdom, where the flight

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: So runs against all reason.

speaker: ROSS
text: My dearest coz,

speaker: ROSS
text: I pray you school yourself. But for your husband,

speaker: ROSS
text: He is noble, wise, judicious, and best knows

speaker: ROSS
text: The fits o’ th’ season. I dare not speak much further;

speaker: ROSS
text: But cruel are the times when we are traitors

speaker: ROSS
text: And do not know ourselves; when we hold rumor

speaker: ROSS
text: From what we fear, yet know not what we fear,

speaker: ROSS
text: But float upon a wild and violent sea

speaker: ROSS
text: Each way and move—I take my leave of you.

speaker: ROSS
text: Shall not be long but I’ll be here again.

speaker: ROSS
text: Things at the worst will cease or else climb upward

speaker: ROSS
text: To what they were before.—My pretty cousin,

speaker: ROSS
text: Blessing upon you.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Fathered he is, and yet he’s fatherless.

speaker: ROSS
text: I am so much a fool, should I stay longer

speaker: ROSS
text: It would be my disgrace and your discomfort.

speaker: ROSS
text: I take my leave at once.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Sirrah, your father’s dead.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: And what will you do now? How will you live?

speaker: SON
text: As birds do, mother.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: What, with worms and flies?

speaker: SON
text: With what I get, I mean; and so do they.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Poor bird, thou ’dst never fear the net nor lime,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: The pitfall nor the gin.

speaker: SON
text: Why should I, mother? Poor birds they are not set for.

speaker: SON
text: My father is not dead, for all your saying.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Yes, he is dead. How wilt thou do for a father?

speaker: SON
text: Nay, how will you do for a husband?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Why, I can buy me twenty at any market.

speaker: SON
text: Then you’ll buy ’em to sell again.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Thou speak’st with all thy wit,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: And yet, i’ faith, with wit enough for thee.

speaker: SON
text: Was my father a traitor, mother?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Ay, that he was.

speaker: SON
text: What is a traitor?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Why, one that swears and lies.

speaker: SON
text: And be all traitors that do so?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Every one that does so is a traitor

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: and must be hanged.

speaker: SON
text: And must they all be hanged that swear and lie?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Every one.

speaker: SON
text: Who must hang them?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Why, the honest men.

speaker: SON
text: Then the liars and swearers are fools, for there

speaker: SON
text: are liars and swearers enough to beat the honest

speaker: SON
text: men and hang up them.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Now God help thee, poor monkey! But

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: how wilt thou do for a father?

speaker: SON
text: If he were dead, you’d weep for him. If you would

speaker: SON
text: not, it were a good sign that I should quickly have a

speaker: SON
text: new father.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Poor prattler, how thou talk’st!

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Bless you, fair dame. I am not to you known,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Though in your state of honor I am perfect.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I doubt some danger does approach you nearly.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: If you will take a homely man’s advice,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Be not found here. Hence with your little ones!

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To fright you thus methinks I am too savage;

speaker: MESSENGER
text: To do worse to you were fell cruelty,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Which is too nigh your person. Heaven preserve you!

speaker: MESSENGER
text: I dare abide no longer.

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Whither should I fly?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: I have done no harm. But I remember now

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: I am in this earthly world, where to do harm

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Is often laudable, to do good sometime

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Accounted dangerous folly. Why then, alas,

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Do I put up that womanly defense

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: To say I have done no harm?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: What are these faces?

speaker: MURDERER
text: Where is your husband?

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: I hope in no place so unsanctified

speaker:
LADY
               
              MACDUFF
text: Where such as thou mayst find him.

speaker: MURDERER
text: He’s a traitor.

speaker: SON
text: Thou liest, thou shag-eared villain!

speaker: MURDERER
text: What, you egg?

speaker: MURDERER
text:
Stabbing
                 
                him
                .
                 
              Young fry of treachery!

speaker: SON
text: He has killed me, mother.

speaker: SON
text: Run away, I pray you.
